title: Over-aggressive pmux2shiftx inferring $sub
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

`./yosys -p "proc; pmux2shiftx; opt -full; stat" pmux2shiftx.v`

```
module top(input [7:0] i, output o);
always @*
case (i[6:3])
    4: o <= i[0];
    3: o <= i[2];
    7: o <= i[3];
    default: o <= 1'b0;
endcase
endmodule
```

Creates:
```
   Number of cells:                  4
     $and                            1
     $shift                          1
     $shiftx                         1
     $sub                            1
```
which includes a `$sub` cell (due to offset correction). This `$sub` cell can get inferred into a tech-specific carry chain from which it can't get optimised away.
![pmux2shiftx](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6740044/60152193-69f30900-9794-11e9-8407-89b9e2ee808b.png)

## Expected behavior

Ideally, ability to create a better `$shiftx` that can be created that will eventually boil down to a 6-input LUT?
`yosys -p "proc; pmux2shiftx; opt -full; techmap; abc -lut 6; stat" pmux2shiftx.v` shows that this is possible.

More concretely: 
`./yosys -p "proc; synth_xilinx -nosrl" pmux2shiftx.v` returns a LUT6 (`-nosrl` flag is necessary otherwise a `pmuxshiftx` is run internally)
but:
`./yosys -p "proc; pmux2shiftx; synth_xilinx -nosrl" pmux2shiftx.v` returns a bigger circuit with multiple LUTs and carry elements.

Otherwise, perhaps option to control `pmux2shiftx`to not perform such conversions?

## Actual behavior

`$sub` cell that gets mapped to a carry chain.

```
3. Executing PMUX2SHIFTX pass.
Inspecting $pmux cell top/$procmux$3.
  data width: 1 (next power-of-2 = 1, log2 = 0)
  checking ctrl signal \i [6:3]
    best permutation: \i [6:3]
    best xor mask: 4'0000
      0: 4'0111 -> 4'0111 -> 4'0111: \i [3]
      1: 4'0011 -> 4'0011 -> 4'0011: \i [2]
      2: 4'0100 -> 4'0100 -> 4'0100: \i [0]
    choices: 3
    min choice: 3
    max choice: 7
    range density: 60%
    absolute density: 37%
    full density: 18%
    full case: false
    offset: 4'0011 <<<<<
    created $shiftx cell $auto$pmux2shiftx.cc:712:execute$12.
```
